Thanks for sharing your video. Build Green Now is a New York base nonprofit. Our goal is to create Innovative solutions.

We welcome an opportunity to collaborate with you on ideas to expand market activity.

Let me know if this interests you.

author: @DeliciousDeBlair
text:
A FLEXIBLE plastic could help manage disposable footwear for emergency rescues, hikers and tourists any place where people want shoes purpose made but do not want to keep them, or which will be for some reason unreusable in the normal ways.

People often go to beach edge resorts and instead of buying flip-flops for the beach at home and packing them, they simply pay a small amount for a pair to use where they go, and then leave them there, where they often end up in the trash.

This is but one of a few possible ways it could be used, assuming that it was not strong enough or durable enough for long life footwear of course. ~( 'w')/

replies.author: @Ineedfreedomnow
replies.text: Hemp plastic is compostable which requires a certain set of conditions (temp, moisture and microbes) before the product would breakdown.  So if we have a product made from hemp plastic it would probably last for years I imagine.
